lines.text: addBehaviour("Page contains a heading that does not exist — assertion fails intentionally");

featureMeta.name: AI Failure Analysis
featureMeta.description: As a developer I want failed tests to be analysed by AI

tests.scenarioDescription: Demonstrates AI analysis on an intentionally failing assertion
behaviours: Standard user logs in successfully
Page contains a heading that does not exist — assertion fails intentionally

steps.title: Expect "toBeVisible" getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })
steps.error:
Error: [2mexpect([22m[31mlocator[39m[2m).[22mtoBeVisible[2m([22m[2m)[22m failed

Locator: getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })
Expected: visible
Timeout: 5000ms
Error: element(s) not found

Call log:
[2m  - Expect "toBeVisible" with timeout 5000ms[22m
[2m  - waiting for getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })[22m

trends.notes: Trend-ready schema. Persist snapshots externally to compute multi-run trends.

aiAnalyses.testName: › chromium › ui/saucedemo.spec.js › AI Failure Analysis › intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression
aiAnalyses.summary: The test intentionally attempts to locate a heading element with the text 'Non Existing Header' using getByRole, which does not exist in the DOM. The assertion toBeVisible() fails after the 5000ms timeout because no such element is present on the page.
aiAnalyses.likelyRootCause: This is a deliberately introduced test failure for demonstration purposes. The locator getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' }) targets a heading that was never rendered in the application, making the failure deterministic and expected by design. The test name explicitly states 'intentional failure for AI analysis demo'.
aiAnalyses.suggestedRemediation: Since this is an intentional demo failure, no production fix is required. However, if this test needs to pass in CI, either: (1) remove or skip the test with test.skip(), (2) replace 'Non Existing Header' with an actual heading present on the page (e.g., 'Products' on the inventory page), or (3) wrap the assertion with expect.soft() if partial failure tolerance is acceptable. If the intent is to keep it as a sentinel failure test, tag it appropriately to exclude it from blocking regression runs.

structuredFeedback.reasoning: The heading name 'Non Existing Header' does not correspond to any element rendered by the SauceDemo application at any point in its UI flow. The failure is 100% reproducible and intentional. The locator strategy itself (getByRole with name) is valid and idiomatic Playwright — the problem is solely the non-existent target string. Updating the heading name to a real value would resolve the failure immediately.

If the intent is to keep it as a sentinel failure test, tag it appropriately to exclude it from blocking regression runs.",
  "issueCategory": "assertion_issue",

    "reasoning": "The heading name 'Non Existing Header' does not correspond to any element rendered by the SauceDemo application at any point in its UI flow. The failure is 100% reproducible and intentional. The locator strategy itself (getByRole with name) is valid and idiomatic Playwright — the problem is solely the non-existent target string. Updating the heading name to a real value would resolve the failure immediately.",
    "suggestedPatch": "// Original (intentionally failing):\nawait expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n\n// Option 1 — Fix to a real heading (e.g., after login on inventory page):\nawait expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });\n\n// Option 2 — Skip the test entirely in CI:\ntest.skip(' intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression', async ({ page }) => {\n  // ...\n});\n\n// Option 3 — Mark as expected failure (xfail pattern):\ntest(' intentional failure for AI analysis demo @regression', async ({ page }) => {\n  await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Non Existing Header' }))\n    .not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 }); // invert assertion to document known absence\n});",
    "candidateLocators": [
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Products' })",
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Swag Labs' })",
      "getByRole('heading', { name: 'Checkout: Your Information' })",
      "locator('.title')",
      "locator('[data-test=\"title\"]')"
    ]
  }
}
